steps.title: Identify two points on the boundary line
steps.content:
The boundary line separates the shaded region from the unshaded region. From the graph, I can identify two clear points where the line intersects the grid:
  • Marked point on the line at (-10, -10)
  • The line crosses the y-axis at (0, -12)

steps.title: Find the slope and equation of the boundary line
steps.content:
With points (-10, -10) and (0, -12):

$$\text{slope} = \frac{-12 - (-10)}{0 - (-10)} = \frac{-2}{10} = -\frac{1}{5}$$

Using point-slope form with (0, -12):
$$y - (-12) = -\frac{1}{5}(x - 0)$$
$$y + 12 = -\frac{1}{5}x$$
$$5y + 60 = -x$$
$$x + 5y = -60$$

The boundary line of the shaded region is $x + 5y = -60$, which represents the equation $rx + ty = -77$ in the standard form where $r$ is the coefficient of $x$ and $t$ is the coefficient of $y$.

steps.title: Extract coefficients and find the sum
steps.content:
From the equation $x + 5y = -60$, we can identify the coefficients of the line as it appears in the graph:
  • $r = 1$ (coefficient of $x$)
  • $t = 5$ (coefficient of $y$)

Therefore:
$$r + t = 1 + 5 = 6$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
